title: Drug Licence Registration

mainHeading: Drug Licence Registration Online – Apply & Renew Pharmacy Licence

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: In India, selling, stocking, exhibiting or distributing drugs without a valid drug licence is a punishable offence under the Drugs and Cosmetics Act, 1940. Whether you are a pharmaceutical company, chemist or a healthcare startup dealing with allopathic, ayurvedic or homeopathic drugs, obtaining a drug licence is mandatory. The Central Drugs Standard Control Organization (CDSCO) and respective State Drug Authorities regulate and issue these licences. Professionals Law Private Limited ensures complete end-to-end assistance for securing the appropriate drug licence—whether it's a retail, wholesale or manufacturing licence—by complying with the latest regulatory guidelines issued under the amended Drugs Rules, 2023.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: A drug licence is legally required to engage in any kind of drug-related business in India. It ensures that the sale, distribution or manufacture of drugs is done under ethical and safe conditions as per government regulations. Obtaining a valid drug licence through Professionals Law prevents legal penalties and guarantees public health protection by allowing only certified professionals to deal with medicines.

body.title: Legal Authorization
body.description: A drug licence grants your business the legal right to manufacture, sell or distribute drugs, ensuring you operate within the law and avoid criminal prosecution under the Drugs and Cosmetics Act, 1940 and related regulatory frameworks.

body.title: Consumer Trust
body.description: Holding a valid drug licence builds customer confidence, showing that your business complies with safety and quality standards. It enhances your credibility in the pharmaceutical and healthcare sector, attracting more loyal and informed consumers to your brand.

body.title: Market Access
body.description: With a drug licence, your business gains entry into formal and regulated markets such as hospitals, pharmacies and institutional buyers. It allows participation in tenders, supply chains and contracts requiring legal certification for dealing in drugs.

body.title: Business Expansion
body.description: A valid drug licence makes it easier to expand operations across different cities or states. It enables faster registration for new branches, partnerships or franchises while maintaining compliance with central and state drug authorities.

body.title: Avoid Regulatory Penalties
body.description: Compliance through a drug licence protects your business from heavy fines, inspections and sudden closures. It ensures smooth operations by preventing legal complications or punitive actions from drug inspectors and state regulatory bodies.

categories.applicantType: To apply for a drug licence in India, the following conditions must be met:
criteria: The applicant must have a suitable premise (minimum 10 sq. meters for retail/wholesale and 15 sq. meters for both).
The premise must have proper storage facilities like refrigeration and ventilation.
A registered pharmacist (for retail) or a competent person with relevant experience (for wholesale) must be appointed.
The applicant must comply with the zoning regulations of the local municipal authority.
The business entity must be registered (as a proprietorship, partnership, LLP or private limited company).

categories.applicantType: Here’s a list of essential documents needed to apply for a drug licence with Professionals Law’s assistance:
documents: System generated application form for relevant drug sale (19/19A/19B/19C as applicable)
Site plan and key plan of the premise
In case of company: MOA, AOA and Copy of resolution Passed;
In case of partnership firm: partnership deed, duly attested by Notary Public;
In case of trust / society: Trust deed;
Photo ID proof of the director / partner / proprietor of the firm.
Affidavit regarding non-conviction of Property/Partner/Director as well as the firm under Drugs & Cosmetics Act, 1940.
Affidavit regarding compliance of MPD 2021, if the premises are located on DDA residential flat / plot /building.
System generated affidavit from the registered pharmacist/competent person.
Proof of qualification i.e. final degree certificate / provisional certificate with mark sheets
If Owned: Registered Sale Deed / Regd. GPA / Conveyance Deed in the name of owner or Unregistered Sale Deed / unregistered GPA supported with Electricity Bill / Water Bill / property tax receipt.
If Rented: Rent receipt, registered rent agreement, unregistered rent agreement along with ownership documents of property owner.
In case of village, Khasra Khatauni documents will be considered

body.description: Professionals Law experts assess your business model and determine the type of licence required.
Our team compiles and verifies all documents for accuracy.
We file your application through the State Drug Control Department's online portal.
Government fees are paid online and confirmation is submitted.
Drug Inspector visits the premises to verify the setup, storage and compliance.
Upon approval, the licence is issued electronically.

body.step: We at Professionals Law also handle follow-ups and corrections (if required) to ensure fast approval.

body.title: Fines which may extend up to ₹5 lakhs or more depending on the offence
Cancellation/Suspension of the existing drug licence
Criminal records and blacklisting for repeated violations
Retail/Wholesale Licence
body.description: The retail/wholesale licence takes around 25 to 30 working days, depending on document readiness and government processing time.

body.title: Manufacturing Licence
body.description: The manufacturing licence takes around 150 to 180 working days, depending on document readiness and government processing time.

body.title: *Delays may occur if documents are incomplete or if inspection reports are unsatisfactory.

body.question: Who issues the drug licence in India?
body.answer: Drug licences are issued by the respective State Drug Control Authority, operating under the provisions of the Drugs and Cosmetics Act, 1940 and the Drugs Rules, 1945.

body.question: Can I run a pharmacy without a drug licence?
body.answer: No. Operating a pharmacy or selling drugs without a valid drug licence is illegal in India and may lead to severe penalties, including fines, imprisonment or closure of your business.

body.question: Is a pharmacist mandatory for getting a retail drug licence?
body.answer: Yes. You must appoint a registered pharmacist with a diploma or degree in pharmacy, approved by the Pharmacy Council, to qualify for a retail drug licence in India.

body.question: What is the validity of a drug licence?
body.answer: Most drug licences are valid for five years. Renewal should be done before expiry to ensure uninterrupted operations and avoid penalties from the drug control department.

body.question: Is GST registration required to apply for a drug licence?
body.answer: Yes, businesses involved in the trade or sale of medicines must have GST registration as it’s a mandatory document for applying for any drug-related licence.

body.question: Can one licence be used for multiple premises?
body.answer: No. Each location or branch dealing in drugs requires a separate drug licence. You must apply individually for every premise involved in sale, storage or distribution.

body.question: How can Professionals Law help in drug licence registration?
body.answer: Professionals Law offers complete support including document preparation, online filing, communication with authorities, inspection scheduling and compliance assistance to ensure you get your drug licence without hassle.

body.question: Can I apply for the licence online myself?
body.answer: Yes, but the process is complex, involving multiple technical documents, regulatory criteria and inspections. Professionals Law simplifies this with expert handling from start to finish for faster approval.

body.question: Is a cold storage facility mandatory for all types of drug licences?
body.answer: Yes, especially for medicines that require controlled temperatures. A refrigerator with a thermostat is mandatory to maintain proper storage and comply with inspection requirements.
